methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $510K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
GenFab Co is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $510K to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector contains nine private peers and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ), including established players like 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, a sufficiently narrow peer set at the same stage and valuation tier does not exist to support median-based valuation.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total capital raised ($510K) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next-stage entry, the implied valuation is $1.28M. This reflects standard progression expectations absent company-specific operating metrics or revenue data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.08M to $1.47M, with a median point of $1.28M. This range rests on a single methodology and lacks the validation of peer-comparable or revenue-based approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of traction metrics, burn rate, and runway—all material inputs that would refine the estimate. The extreme sparsity of same-stage comparables in this subsector means any valuation should be treated as preliminary and subject to material revision upon disclosure of operating performance or capital requirements for the next funding round.
